title: Building an Agent Loop
tagline: The core of turbo-agent-kit, no framework, ~300 lines

beats.say: I just merged the core of an agent loop. No framework, about three hundred lines. Let me walk through how an LLM actually uses tools.
beats.show: An agent loop is a conversation plus a controller that decides when to stop.

beats.say: Everything is a list of messages. A user message goes in, and the orchestrator owns the turn from there.
beats.show: One conversation is an ordered list of messages: user, assistant, tool.

beats.say: Each step it hands the whole message history to the model and asks one question: give me a final answer, or a tool call.
beats.show: Every step asks the model: final text, or call a tool?

beats.say: If it wants a tool, the name is looked up in a registry and the arguments are validated with zod before a single line runs.
beats.show: Tool calls resolve by name in a registry; args are zod-validated first.

beats.say: The tool result gets pushed back into the conversation and the loop runs again. It is capped at five steps so it can never spin forever.
beats.show: Tool output feeds back in; the loop repeats, capped at 5 steps.

beats.say: Two things I bolted on. Telemetry times every model and tool call, and an onMessage hook streams each message so a UI can show the turn as it happens.
beats.show: Telemetry times every call; onMessage streams the turn live.
